metadata.title:
FIND DYNAMITE
CONCEALED BY


ROBBERS
Officers Watch in Vain for
Return of Man Who


Hid Explosive.

pages.raw_text:
The cache OF the Great Northern
train holdups has been discovered.


Several days ago Sergeant Nc
Phee and Superintendent A. w.
Stauffer of the merchants police
were informed that box of dyna-
mite was hid away in small rock
cave near the International tracks
at the intersection of Division
street


Circumstances indicated that 1t
was the explosive the bandits had
hidden there. intending to use it
as a base Of operations in other
holdups. Just 16 sticks of the high
explosive were missing out of the
box. The dynamite charges, all
fused and capped, which the ban
dts left In the mail car they
robbed, contained 16 sticks. This
led the officers to the belief that
the hidden dynamite belonged to
the bandits.


The cashe was watched for sew
eral days without anyone appear
ing on the scene. Yesterday the
place was thoroughly investigated
and loose rocks overturned In
hopes that the money stolen from
the registered mail would be dis
e covered.


The only other suspicious article
found was railroad torpedo
which railroad men use In stopping
trains by attaching to the rails. 1t
is presumed the bandits were
planning another holdup and would
have used the torpedo as signal
that there was danger ahead, and
when the engineer stopped the
train would board the engin and
cover those In the cab with re-
volvers and repeat the Great
Northern job.


The proximity of the cache to
the International tracks leads to
the belief that it was planned to
hold up either an International or
an O. r. & N. train.


Following the holdup posse
tracked the bandits in the fresh
snow to Division street, where all
trace was lost on account of the
condition of the street. which had
been Travelled by others.


1t Is believed that the bandits
are still In the city. Local police
do not associate them with the
holdup Of tn 60. II. Il N. near
Portland last night.


Working on the clew furnished
In discovery of the cache, Ser
geant McPhee has kept officers in
plain clothes stationed in the vi
cinity for several days. Had any
one approached the cache while
the officers were on guard cap
ture or killing would have been
certain, for the officers were
heavily armed.
